title: Doe receipt provenance
description: Schema for the PROVENANCE.json files that sit alongside each artifact directory under bench/out/r3-1-*. Each provenance file records: schema version, artifact kind, promotion timestamp, human-readable purpose, a claim (scope + notWhat) that scopes what the receipt does and does not assert, and either `produced` (when the receipt was produced fresh by a runner) or `promoted` (when the receipt was copied/promoted from a dated source dir). Optional `reviewerNotes` carry instructions for external reviewers (e.g. how to obtain bundled-out source artifacts).

schemaVersion.description: Semantic version of the provenance schema. Currently 1.

artifactKind.description: Free-form snake_case identifier naming the receipt class (e.g. `doe_31b_real_weight_pin_provenance`). Used by aggregation tooling to group receipts.

promotedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp at which the artifact was promoted to its stable bundle path. Records the moment of promotion, not the moment the underlying evidence was produced.

purpose.description: One-paragraph explanation of why this receipt exists and what it is evidence for. Reviewer-facing.

claim.description: Strict scope of what this receipt asserts. `scope` names what the receipt proves; `notWhat` names what the receipt explicitly does NOT prove. Both required so reviewers cannot mistake bounded evidence for a stronger claim.

produced.description: Optional. Records the runner / wrapper / inputs that produced the receipt fresh. Mutually exclusive in spirit with `promoted` but both allowed for hybrid cases.

promoted.description: Optional. Records the source-side dated path(s) that the artifact was copied from, with hashes pinning identity.

reviewerNotes.description: Optional reviewer-facing instructions: how to obtain the source artifact, how to reproduce locally, how to verify identity. Used when the local path in `produced` or `promoted` is not addressable by an external reviewer.
